title: pandanus tectorius var. laevigatus
intro: Pandanus tectorius var. laevigatus, also known as the screw pine or pandan, is a type of plant that belongs to the Pandanus genus and is native to the Pacific Island region. The leaves and roots of the pandan plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of pandan include reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and providing relief from digestive issues and skin conditions. The herbal preparations of pandan include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are often used to make teas, tonics, and creams for topical application.
